{"contents":"<mvc:View
	controllerName="com.demo.migration.projects.legacy.controller.Master"
	xmlns="sap.m"
	xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
	xmlns:core="sap.ui.core">
	<Page
		id="masterPage"
		title="{i18n>masterTitle}"
		showNavButton="false">

		<subHeader>
			<Toolbar>
				<SearchField
					id="searchField"
					width="100%"
					placeholder="{i18n>masterSearchPlaceholder}"
					liveChange="onSearch" />
			</Toolbar>
		</subHeader>

		<content>
			<List
				id="projectList"
				items="{
					path: '/ProjectSet',
					sorter: { path: 'StartDate', descending: true }
				}"
				mode="SingleSelectMaster"
				growing="true"
				growingThreshold="20"
				selectionChange="onSelect"
				noDataText="No projects found.">
				<ObjectListItem
					title="{Title}"
					number="{ path: 'Status', formatter: 'window.com.demo.migration.projects.legacy.model.formatter.statusText' }"
					numberState="{ path: 'Status', formatter: 'window.com.demo.migration.projects.legacy.model.formatter.statusState' }"
					type="Active"
					press="onItemPress">
					<firstStatus>
						<ObjectStatus
							text="ID: {ProjectId}" />
					</firstStatus>
					<attributes>
						<ObjectAttribute text="{ path: 'StartDate', formatter: 'window.com.demo.migration.projects.legacy.model.formatter.dateShort' } – { path: 'EndDate', formatter: 'window.com.demo.migration.projects.legacy.model.formatter.dateShort' }" />
						<ObjectAttribute text="{Description}" />
					</attributes>
				</ObjectListItem>
			</List>
		</content>

		<footer>
			<Toolbar>
				<Text id="countText" text="" />
				<ToolbarSpacer />
			</Toolbar>
		</footer>
	</Page>
</mvc:View>
","totalLinesInFile":59}

        Retrieves a list of user stored SAP Systems and matches the name, host or a URL to the sapSystemQuery provided by the user.

detailedLines.text: | Generator | **SAP Fiori MCP server (`@sap-ux/fiori-mcp-server`)** via the `list_functionalities` → `get_functionality_details` → `execute_functionality` sequence | First-party SAP tool; understands FE V4 patterns natively |
| App namespace | `<source_namespace>.fe` | Keeps the legacy and FE apps distinguishable (and the modern TS app too, if `modernize-ui5-app` was also run as the alternate path) |
| UI5 version | Latest 1.x release (e.g. `1.147.2`) unless user specifies | LTS-track; latest FE V4 features available |
| Language | TypeScript | Modern default; extension files use type-safe APIs |

| OData V4 service URL | **Prefer the SRVD-direct path** on the system's HTTPS port: `https://<host>:<https-port>/sap/opu/odata4/sap/<srvb>/srvd/sap/<srvb>/0001` (e.g. `https://example-s4hana.local:50001/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/srvd/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/0001`). Falls back to the Gateway hub path `http://<host>:<http-port>/sap/opu/odata4/sap/<srvb>/srvd_a2x/sap/<service>/0001` only when SRVD-direct isn't reachable. | Run 5 + Run 6 verified the SRVD-direct path works without `/n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE` registration on 7.5x systems. The hub path requires the routing-group manual step. |
| Main entity | Root entity alias exposed by the SRVB (e.g. the alias on `define root view entity ... alias <X>`) | The LR+OP floorplan is rooted at a single entity |

| Annotations location | **In CDS via `SAPWrite update DDLS`** — not in a local annotation file inside the FE app | The annotations belong to the service; FE app reads them through `$metadata`. Local annotation files are an antipattern for RAP-bound apps. |
| Extension language | TypeScript (controller extensions) | Match the rest of the chain |
| Validation | `mcp__SAPUI5_MCP_Server__run_ui5_linter` + `run_manifest_validation` + `tsc --noEmit` all green | The hard acceptance criteria |
| Acceptance | FE app runs end-to-end against the V4 service. Browser smoke covers every feature inventoried in Phase 1 — reproduced through annotations or extension hooks. | Concrete deliverable |
- **Path** to the legacy app for feature mining (default: `<source_app>/`). Read-only.
- (Optional) SAPWrite(action=update, type=BDEF, ...) + SAPActivate for BDEF precheck
| `$metadata` reflects no annotations after `SAPWrite update DDLS` | DDLS reactivated but SRVB wasn't republished | `SAPWrite(action="publish_srvb", name="<V4_SRVB>")` |

| `mcp__fiori-mcp__list_functionalities` returns empty / fails | Server not registered, or `npx` cache stale | Verify `.cursor/mcp.json`; in a fresh shell, `npx --yes @sap-ux/fiori-mcp-server@latest fiori-mcp` should print server-ready logs |

| Generator fails with "service unreachable" | The `<V4_service_URL>` is gated by 403 because of the V4 routing group | Surface the `/n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE` manual step from `migrate-segw-to-rap.md` Phase 6 |

| Generator succeeds but FE app shows a blank shell | `mainEntity` passed without annotated CDS | Re-check Phase 4 — every entity in the `@UI.Facet` chain needs at least `@UI.LineItem` and (for OP root) `@UI.HeaderInfo` |
| List-report shows no columns | `@UI.LineItem` annotation is on the root view, not the projection | Move annotations to the projection; FE reads them from the SRVB-exposed projection |

| Action button not visible | Action wasn't exposed on the projection BDEF, or projection BDEF missing `use action approve_project` | Check projection BDEF (`define behavior for <root_projection> { ... use action approve_project; }`) and re-publish |

| Filter bar empty | `@UI.SelectionFields` annotation missing or pointing at field that's not marked `@Search.searchable` at view level | Add `@Search.searchable: true` at the view level and `@Search.defaultSearchElement: true` on the right fields |
| Acceptance | FE app runs end-to-end against the V4 service. Browser smoke covers every feature inventoried in Phase 1 — reproduced through annotations or extension hooks. | Concrete deliverable |
- **Path** to the legacy app for feature mining (default: `<source_app>/`). Read-only.
- **OData V4 service URL** — derived from `migrate-segw-to-rap`'s SRVB output. Required.
- **Root entity alias** (e.g. `Project`). Default: infer from the SRVB's `$metadata`.
- **Transport** for the CDS annotation writes (default: same transport used by
If only the legacy app path and V4 URL are provided, apply smart defaults and surface the plan
in Phase 3 for user `ok` before any writes.
### 0a. RAP V4 service is live and bindable
Assert the SRVB exists and is active. If not, stop with *"V4 service binding `<V4_SRVB>` is
Assert 200 OK. **Try the SRVD-direct path first** (HTTPS port + `/srvd/sap/<srvb>/0001/`).
If 403 with `IWBEP/CM_V4_COS/136`, the URL is using the Gateway hub path and the routing
group isn't registered. **Switch to the SRVD-direct URL pattern before stopping** — the
direct path bypasses the hub registration. Only fall back to *"register service group via
If 503 with `IWBEP/CM_V4_RUNTIME/000` ("service alias cache outdated"), retry once — this is
a transient Gateway cache issue. If it persists across retries, treat as 403 and switch URL
### 3e. Warning — the scaffold itself uses Hungarian notation
The UI5 MCP scaffold templates (`Component.ts`, `BaseController.ts`, the default
`*.controller.ts`, `models.ts`) ship with Hungarian-prefixed parameter and local-variable
names (`sName`, `oModel`, `oParameters`, etc.). The Naming overrides section above applies to
the scaffold too — rename them as you read each scaffolded file, not just when porting from
The fastest workflow: scaffold first, then do **one batch grep+rename pass** over the
scaffolded `webapp/` for the half-dozen common prefixes (`oModel`, `sName`, `oEvent`,
`oParameters`, etc.) BEFORE writing any new per-view controllers. This way the BaseController
you're about to extend is already in modern style.
Read `<target>/webapp/manifest.json`, then **merge in** the legacy specifics:

`mcp__sap-docs__search` for the V4 binding pattern your backend uses; SRVD-direct on ABAP
If all three pass, print **"Phase 0 ✓ Authorizations OK — proceeding to discovery."** and continue.
If user gave a service name (e.g. `<legacy_service>`):
- Try convention: trim `_SRV` → append `_MPC` → `<MPC_class>`. Verify with
SAPQuery(action="sql", sql="SELECT obj_name, devclass FROM tadir
AND obj_name LIKE 'Z%_MPC' ORDER BY obj_name")
and ask the user to pick.
If user gave an MPC class directly: use it as-is.
Determine the corresponding `_MPC_EXT`, `_DPC`, `_DPC_EXT` by name convention.
### 1b. Confirm the four classes exist + record metadata
## Phase 2 — Extract the OData model from MPC source
The MPC class's private `DEFINE_*` methods carry the full model. Read them.
- Entity type name + entity set name (look for `create_entity_type(...)` and
- Underlying ABAP DDIC bind (look for `bind_structure(...)` or `set_data_source(...)`)
- **`fetch-service-metadata` returns an MCP framework error instead of a structured response**
when the `@sap-ux/store` has no entry for the target system. Workaround: `curl $metadata`
yourself to a local file (e.g. `<fe_app>/metadata.xml`), then point `execute_functionality`
closes, the curl workaround can be dropped.
- **`fetch-service-metadata` requires Connection Manager configuration** for stored
system entries; direct URL passthrough to `execute_functionality` bypasses this. Skill's
Phase 0e already documents the direct-URL path.
Capture any **new** rough edges in `RUN-NOTES.md` so the next iteration can either work
around them or file follow-up upstream issues.

result: {"result":"{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"{\\\"id\\\":\\\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"abap-fiori-showcase > 05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"**Source:** ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE Documentation\\\\n**URL:** https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\\\\n**File:** 05_object_page_header.md\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n# Object Page - Header Area\\\\n\\\\n## Content\\\\n- [Object Page - Header Area](#object-page---header-area)\\\\n - [Title and Subtitle](#title-and-subtitle)\\\\n - [Header Facets](#header-facets)\\\\n - [Header Field Group Facet](#header-field-group-facet)\\\\n - [Handling Semantic Key Fields](#handling-semantic-key-fields)\\\\n - [Plain Text](#plain-text)\\\\n - [Address Facet](#address-facet)\\\\n - [Data Points](#data-points)\\\\n - [Rating](#rating)\\\\n - [Progress](#progress)\\\\n - [Key Value](#key-value)\\\\n - [Micro Chart Facet](#micro-chart-facet)\\\\n - [Area Micro Chart](#area-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Bullet Micro Chart](#bullet-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Radial Micro Chart](#radial-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Line Micro Chart](#line-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Column Micro Chart](#column-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Harvey Micro Chart](#harvey-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Stacked Bar Micro Chart](#stacked-bar-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Comparison Micro Chart](#comparison-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Actions - Object Page Header Area](#actions---object-page-header-area)\\\\n - [Copy Action in Object Page](#copy-action-in-object-page)\\\\n - [Regular Actions](#regular-actions)\\\\n - [Hide Standard Operations](#hide-standard-operations)\\\\n - [Datafield WithAction in Object Page](#datafield-withaction-in-object-page)\\\\n\\\\n## Title and Subtitle\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderInfo`\\\\n\\\\nThe title and subtitle of an Object Page are defined with the annotation `@UI.headerInfo`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderInfo\\\\n@UI.headerInfo: {\\\\n typeName: 'Object Page - Root',\\\\n typeNamePlural: 'Root Entities (#ListTableTitle)', // Search Term #ListTableTitle\\\\n typeImageUrl: 'sap-icon://sales-order',\\\\n imageUrl: 'ImageUrl',\\\\n title: {\\\\n value: 'StringProperty',\\\\n type: #STANDARD\\\\n },\\\\n description: {\\\\n label: 'Root entity',\\\\n type: #STANDARD,\\\\n value: 'StringProperty'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe attribute `typeName` is the title and it is displayed next to the SAP Logo in header bar on the Object Page. The attribute `typeNamePlural` will be shown, if all entities are shown in a table. The attribute `title` is displayed as the header in the Object Page in bold. Attribute `description` is shown beneath `title` and if the optional `imageUrl` is given, then the picture will be visible on the left side of the `title` and `description`. If no url is given for the `imageUrl`, but `typeImageUrl` is defined, it will be displayed instead.\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: \\\\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Table Header of a List Report](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-table-header-of-list-report)\\\\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Title Section of an Object Page](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-title-section-of-object-page)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Header Facets\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFacet`\\\\n\\\\nThe header facet is displayed in the header of an Object Page. The facet is annotated with `UI.facet.purpose: #HEADER`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n // OPHEADER\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderFacet)'\\\\n}\\\\nFieldWithPrice;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Using Facets to change the Object Page Layout](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/using-facets-to-change-object-page-layout)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Header Field Group Facet\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFieldGroup`, `#ContactInHeader`, `#OPQuickView`\\\\n\\\\nField groups can be part of a header.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER\\\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA quick view contact card can be displayed (#ContactInHeader). A QuickView can be displayed by just adding the property as a data field (#OPQuickView). Both implementations are identical to how they would be added as a line item in a List Report:\\\\n- [Adding a Contact Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-contact-quick-view-to-a-table)\\\\n- [Adding a Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-quick-view-to-a-table)\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n position: 10,\\\\n label: 'Field with Sem. Key(#SemanticKey)'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nStringProperty;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #ContactInHeader, #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n type: #AS_CONTACT,\\\\n label: 'Contact QuickView (#ContactInHeader)',\\\\n position: 70,\\\\n value: '_Contact'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nContactID;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithAction\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n position: 20,\\\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithAction)',\\\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #OPQuickView\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n position: 50,\\\\n label: 'Navigation QuickView (#OPQuickView)'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nNavigationID;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Handling Semantic Key Fields\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#SemanticKey`\\\\n\\\\nSemantic Key fields can be defined, with the annotation `@ObjectModel.semanticKey`, which consists of an array of properties from the entity. The given fields will be displayed in bold, and when possible the editing status will be displayed. \\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #SemanticKey\\\\n@ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['StringProperty']\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Plain Text\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#PlainText`\\\\n\\\\nPlain text can be displayed, by adding a standard property to a field group and using this field group as a target of a reference facet.\\\\n\\\\nThe property in the field group can be optionally annotated with [`@UI.MultiLineText`](/04_object_page_general.md#multi-line-text), especially if you have a long text and would like it to be show in the UI as a multi-line text, with an expand/collapse button.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #PlainText\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\\\n label: 'Plain Text (#PlainText)',\\\\n targetQualifier: 'plainText'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n // Search Term #PlainText\\\\n fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'plainText' } ]\\\\n // Search Term #MultiLineText\\\\n multiLineText: true\\\\n}\\\\nDescription;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Grouping Fields](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/grouping-fields)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Address Facet\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#AddressFacet`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe property of an entity annotated with `@Semantics.address.label` can be directly displayed in an address facet, with target qualifier **'pref'** if no specific qualifier is set. The label will be used as is, therefore it needs to be fully formatted. Linebreaks can be achieved with '\\\\\\\\n'.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_I_Contact**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #CONTACT\\\\ndefine view entity /DMO/FSA_I_Contact\\\\n...\\\\n{\\\\n ...\\\\n @Semantics.address.label: true\\\\n @Consumption.valueHelpDefault.display:false \\\\n address_label as AddressLabel,\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #AddressFacet\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\\\n label : 'Address (#AddressFacet)',\\\\n type : #ADDRESS_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetElement: '_Contact',\\\\n targetQualifier: 'pref'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Data Points\\\\n\\\\n### Rating\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointRating`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nTo add a rating indicator (stars) to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets how many stars are visible. Values between x.25 and x.74 are displayed as a half star. The attribute `targetValue` defines how many stars are possible. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI: { \\\\n // Search Term #DataPointRating, #DataPointRatingTable\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'StarsValue',\\\\n targetValue: 4,\\\\n visualization: #RATING,\\\\n title: 'Rating Indicator (#DataPointRating)'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nStarsValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n // Search Term #DataPointRating\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'StarsValue'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Rating](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Progress\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointProgress`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nTo add a progress indicator to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets the current progress and the attribute `targetValue`, the maximum progress. Additionally, a criticality can be given, if wanted. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #DataPointProgress, #DataPointProgressTable\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue',\\\\n targetValue: 100,\\\\n visualization: #PROGRESS,\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n title: 'Progress Indicator (#DataPointProgress)'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nProgressIntegerValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n // Search Term #DataPointProgress\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Progress](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Key Value\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#KeyValue`\\\\n\\\\nA key value is the default data point, when the attribute `visualization` is not specified in `@UI.dataPoint`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n // Search Term #Headerfacet, #KeyValue\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderDataPoint)'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nFieldWithPrice;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Micro Chart Facet\\\\n\\\\nThe following micro charts are supported: Area, Bullet, Radial, Column, Line, Harvey, Stacked bar and Comparison. Micro charts can only be displayed in the header. \\\\n\\\\nA micro chart is defined with the `@UI.chart` annotation, which then is the target of a ReferenceFacet in the `@UI.facet` of `purpose: #HEADER`. The title of the facet is the attribute `title` of the annotation and the subtitle is `description`. If the property of the data point is a property with unit of measure, the unit will be displayed in the footer. The attribute `measures` of the chart has to be a data point. \\\\n\\\\nThe `@UI.dataPoint` supports generally the attributes `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation`, but the support varies between the micro chart types. If the value of the data point is annotated with a unit of measure, the unit will be shown as the footer of the micro chart facet.\\\\n\\\\nIn the following examples, all used attributes are mandatory.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Area Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#AreaMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe area micro chart is a trend chart, which provides information for the actual and target value for a specified dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the dimension. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the measure attribute.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n // Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'areaChart',\\\\n title: 'Area Micro Chart (#AreaMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #AREA,\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForAreaChart'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe criticality calculation of the data point is mandatory, as each value is shown with its threshold (error, warning, acceptance and good) ranges.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue',\\\\n criticalityCalculation: { \\\\n improvementDirection: #TARGET,\\\\n toleranceRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartTolLowerboundValue',\\\\n toleranceRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartTolUpperboundValue',\\\\n deviationRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartDevUpperboundValue',\\\\n deviationRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartDevLowerboundValue'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForAreaChart;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'areaChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/BTP/923180ddb98240829d935862025004d6/c7f12219d533404f8fad96aa68fa4ba6.html)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Bullet Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#BulletMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe bullet chart features a single measure and compares it to one or more other measures (e.g. value with target comparison). Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. The bullet chart does not support the criticality value of 5 (new item). The `measuresAttributes`, while it is mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\nChart\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart, #BulletMicroChartTable\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'bulletChart',\\\\n title: 'Bullet Micro Chart (#BulletMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #BULLET,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValue'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'IntegerValue',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFacet\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'bulletChart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. The attribute `minimumValue` is needed to render the chart properly. The value is the actual bar. The `forecastValue` is the bar in the background with a lower opacity and the `targetValue` is the dark line. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term: #BulletMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValue', //IntegerValue: horizontal bar in relation to the goal line\\\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //visual goal line in the UI\\\\n forecastValue: 'ForecastValue', //horizontal bar behind the value bar with, slightly larger with higher transparency\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n minimumValue: 0 //Minimal value, needed for output rendering\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Radial Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#RadialMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe radial micro chart displays a single percentage value. The `measureAttributes`, while mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\nChart\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'radialChart',\\\\n title: 'Radial Micro Chart (#RadialMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #DONUT,\\\\n measures: ['RadialIntegerValue'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFacet\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'radialChart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. The percentage value is the fraction of the property value and the target value. The unit of measure label will not be rendered, as the chart displays percentage values. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //The relation between the value and the target value will be displayed as a percentage\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nRadialIntegerValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Line Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#LineMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe line chart displays a series of data points as a line. The bottom values are the border values of the `dimension`. The upper left value is the smallest value of the first `measures` property and the upper right value is the largest value of the last `measures` property. The shown unit of measure is from the first entry.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'lineChart',\\\\n title: 'Line Micro Chart (#LineMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #LINE,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForLineChart', 'TargetValue'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n },\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'TargetValue',\\\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Points. It is recommended to only use one measure, and a maximum of three measures, if more is required. If the attribute `criticality` contains a path, then the value of the last data point's `criticality` determines the color of the line.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'TargetValue',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nTargetValue;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForLineChart;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'lineChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Column Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#ColumnMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA column chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'columnChart',\\\\n title: 'Column Micro Chart (#ColumnMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #COLUMN,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'columnChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Harvey Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HarveyMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA harvey chart plots a single measure value against a maximum value.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\nChart\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'harveyChart',\\\\n title: 'Harvey Micro Chart (#HarveyMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #PIE,\\\\n measures: ['HarveyFieldWithPrice'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. For the semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n//Search-Term: #HarveyMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\\\n maximumValue: 5000,\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nHarveyFieldWithPrice;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFacet\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'harveyChart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Stacked Bar Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#StackedBarMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA stacked bar chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [ {\\\\n qualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\\\n title: 'StackedBar Micro Chart (#StackedBarMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #BAR_STACKED,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n}]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Comparison Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#ComparisionMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA comparison chart uses three horizontal bars to compare values of a dimension. If more values are defined in `dimensions`, they will only show up in the tooltip. The displayed values on the left represent the dimension value of each data point. The values on the right are the actual values. If a unit of measure is shown, then it is from the first data point to be plotted.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\\\n title: 'Comparison Micro Chart (#ComparisonMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #BAR,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. For semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Actions - Object Page Header Area\\\\n\\\\n### Copy Action in Object Page\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#OPCopyAction`\\\\n\\\\n> [!WARNING] \\\\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nFor a deep copy of an instance, the RAP framework does not provide a copy action out of the box, unlike create/edit/delete. However you can define a factory action in the BDEF that copies an instance, and by using the keyword `isCopyAction: true`, the action button will be rendered in UI harmoniously with Edit/Delete.\\\\n\\\\nThe cardinality of a factory action is always [1], that means, only one instance can be copied at a time.\\\\n\\\\nWhen no label is provided, it is automatically set to 'Copy'. \\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.identification: [\\\\n // Search Term #OPCopyAction\\\\n {\\\\n type:#FOR_ACTION,\\\\n dataAction: 'copyInstance',\\\\n isCopyAction: true\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nfactory action copyInstance [1];\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Regular Actions\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#OPHeaderAction`\\\\n\\\\nActions for the Object Page in general are annotated using `@UI.identification`. The criticality only supports the values 0 (normal), 1 (red) and 3 (green). For normal actions the Object Page content is passed and for static actions, no context is passed. \\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #OPHeaderAction\\\\n@UI.identification: [\\\\n {\\\\n type: #FOR_ACTION, //Action in the RootEntities of the object page next to the edit button\\\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#OPHeaderAction)',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n criticalityRepresentation: #WITH_ICON,\\\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nID;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Action Implementation - UI Consumption of actions](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/action-implementation#ui-consumption-of-actions)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Hide Standard Operations\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#DynamicCRUD`\\\\n\\\\n> [!WARNING] \\\\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe visibility of the \\\\\\\"Edit\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"Create\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\"Delete\\\\\\\" buttons in the Object Page can be dynamically adjusted. For example the delete operation can be dependent on a property of the entity, through the annotation `@UI.deleteHidden`. Fixed values true or false are also possible.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #DynamicCRUD\\\\n@UI.updateHidden: #(UpdateHidden)\\\\n@UI.deleteHidden: #(DeleteHidden)\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Datafield WithAction in Object Page\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#WithActionInOP`\\\\n\\\\n> [!WARNING] \\\\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nAn action that is tied to a data value, which would be rendered as a hyperlink. Therefore it is crucial to specify the annotation at the desired element which has the data value.\\\\n\\\\nThe keyword to use for this is `type: #WITH_ACTION`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderCollectionFacet\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n id: 'FacetCollection',\\\\n type: #COLLECTION\\\\n },\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n {\\\\n parentId : 'FacetCollection',\\\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n fieldGroup: [\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithActionInOP\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n position: 30,\\\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithActionInOP)',\\\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n}\\\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*This content is from the ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE documentation. Visit the URL above for the latest version and interactive examples.*\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\\\",\\\"metadata\\\":{\\\"source\\\":\\\"abap-docs\\\",\\\"library\\\":\\\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\\\",\\\"contentLength\\\":34889}}\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"id\":\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"title\":\"abap-fiori-showcase > 05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"text\":\"**Source:** ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE Documentation\\n**URL:** https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\\n**File:** 05_object_page_header.md\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Object Page - Header Area\\n\\n## Content\\n- [Object Page - Header Area](#object-page---header-area)\\n - [Title and Subtitle](#title-and-subtitle)\\n - [Header Facets](#header-facets)\\n - [Header Field Group Facet](#header-field-group-facet)\\n - [Handling Semantic Key Fields](#handling-semantic-key-fields)\\n - [Plain Text](#plain-text)\\n - [Address Facet](#address-facet)\\n - [Data Points](#data-points)\\n - [Rating](#rating)\\n - [Progress](#progress)\\n - [Key Value](#key-value)\\n - [Micro Chart Facet](#micro-chart-facet)\\n - [Area Micro Chart](#area-micro-chart)\\n - [Bullet Micro Chart](#bullet-micro-chart)\\n - [Radial Micro Chart](#radial-micro-chart)\\n - [Line Micro Chart](#line-micro-chart)\\n - [Column Micro Chart](#column-micro-chart)\\n - [Harvey Micro Chart](#harvey-micro-chart)\\n - [Stacked Bar Micro Chart](#stacked-bar-micro-chart)\\n - [Comparison Micro Chart](#comparison-micro-chart)\\n - [Actions - Object Page Header Area](#actions---object-page-header-area)\\n - [Copy Action in Object Page](#copy-action-in-object-page)\\n - [Regular Actions](#regular-actions)\\n - [Hide Standard Operations](#hide-standard-operations)\\n - [Datafield WithAction in Object Page](#datafield-withaction-in-object-page)\\n\\n## Title and Subtitle\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderInfo`\\n\\nThe title and subtitle of an Object Page are defined with the annotation `@UI.headerInfo`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderInfo\\n@UI.headerInfo: {\\n typeName: 'Object Page - Root',\\n typeNamePlural: 'Root Entities (#ListTableTitle)', // Search Term #ListTableTitle\\n typeImageUrl: 'sap-icon://sales-order',\\n imageUrl: 'ImageUrl',\\n title: {\\n value: 'StringProperty',\\n type: #STANDARD\\n },\\n description: {\\n label: 'Root entity',\\n type: #STANDARD,\\n value: 'StringProperty'\\n }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe attribute `typeName` is the title and it is displayed next to the SAP Logo in header bar on the Object Page. The attribute `typeNamePlural` will be shown, if all entities are shown in a table. The attribute `title` is displayed as the header in the Object Page in bold. Attribute `description` is shown beneath `title` and if the optional `imageUrl` is given, then the picture will be visible on the left side of the `title` and `description`. If no url is given for the `imageUrl`, but `typeImageUrl` is defined, it will be displayed instead.\\n\\nMore Information: \\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Table Header of a List Report](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-table-header-of-list-report)\\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Title Section of an Object Page](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-title-section-of-object-page)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Header Facets\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFacet`\\n\\nThe header facet is displayed in the header of an Object Page. The facet is annotated with `UI.facet.purpose: #HEADER`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // OPHEADER\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderFacet)'\\n}\\nFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Using Facets to change the Object Page Layout](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/using-facets-to-change-object-page-layout)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Header Field Group Facet\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFieldGroup`, `#ContactInHeader`, `#OPQuickView`\\n\\nField groups can be part of a header.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nA quick view contact card can be displayed (#ContactInHeader). A QuickView can be displayed by just adding the property as a data field (#OPQuickView). Both implementations are identical to how they would be added as a line item in a List Report:\\n- [Adding a Contact Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-contact-quick-view-to-a-table)\\n- [Adding a Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-quick-view-to-a-table)\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n position: 10,\\n label: 'Field with Sem. Key(#SemanticKey)'\\n }\\n]\\nStringProperty;\\n\\n// Search Term #ContactInHeader, #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n type: #AS_CONTACT,\\n label: 'Contact QuickView (#ContactInHeader)',\\n position: 70,\\n value: '_Contact'\\n }\\n]\\nContactID;\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithAction\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n position: 20,\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithAction)',\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\n }\\n]\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #OPQuickView\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n position: 50,\\n label: 'Navigation QuickView (#OPQuickView)'\\n }\\n]\\nNavigationID;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Handling Semantic Key Fields\\n\\n*Search term:* `#SemanticKey`\\n\\nSemantic Key fields can be defined, with the annotation `@ObjectModel.semanticKey`, which consists of an array of properties from the entity. The given fields will be displayed in bold, and when possible the editing status will be displayed. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #SemanticKey\\n@ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['StringProperty']\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Plain Text\\n\\n*Search term:* `#PlainText`\\n\\nPlain text can be displayed, by adding a standard property to a field group and using this field group as a target of a reference facet.\\n\\nThe property in the field group can be optionally annotated with [`@UI.MultiLineText`](/04_object_page_general.md#multi-line-text), especially if you have a long text and would like it to be show in the UI as a multi-line text, with an expand/collapse button.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #PlainText\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n label: 'Plain Text (#PlainText)',\\n targetQualifier: 'plainText'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n // Search Term #PlainText\\n fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'plainText' } ]\\n // Search Term #MultiLineText\\n multiLineText: true\\n}\\nDescription;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Grouping Fields](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/grouping-fields)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Address Facet\\n\\n*Search term:* `#AddressFacet`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe property of an entity annotated with `@Semantics.address.label` can be directly displayed in an address facet, with target qualifier **'pref'** if no specific qualifier is set. The label will be used as is, therefore it needs to be fully formatted. Linebreaks can be achieved with '\\\\n'.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_I_Contact**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #CONTACT\\ndefine view entity /DMO/FSA_I_Contact\\n...\\n{\\n ...\\n @Semantics.address.label: true\\n @Consumption.valueHelpDefault.display:false \\n address_label as AddressLabel,\\n}\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AddressFacet\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n label : 'Address (#AddressFacet)',\\n type : #ADDRESS_REFERENCE,\\n targetElement: '_Contact',\\n targetQualifier: 'pref'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Data Points\\n\\n### Rating\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointRating`\\n\\n\\n\\nTo add a rating indicator (stars) to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets how many stars are visible. Values between x.25 and x.74 are displayed as a half star. The attribute `targetValue` defines how many stars are possible. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI: { \\n // Search Term #DataPointRating, #DataPointRatingTable\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'StarsValue',\\n targetValue: 4,\\n visualization: #RATING,\\n title: 'Rating Indicator (#DataPointRating)'\\n }\\n}\\nStarsValue;\\n```\\n\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n // Search Term #DataPointRating\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'StarsValue'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Rating](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Progress\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointProgress`\\n\\n\\n\\nTo add a progress indicator to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets the current progress and the attribute `targetValue`, the maximum progress. Additionally, a criticality can be given, if wanted. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #DataPointProgress, #DataPointProgressTable\\n@UI: {\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue',\\n targetValue: 100,\\n visualization: #PROGRESS,\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n title: 'Progress Indicator (#DataPointProgress)'\\n }\\n}\\nProgressIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n // Search Term #DataPointProgress\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Progress](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Key Value\\n\\n*Search term:* `#KeyValue`\\n\\nA key value is the default data point, when the attribute `visualization` is not specified in `@UI.dataPoint`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // Search Term #Headerfacet, #KeyValue\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderDataPoint)'\\n }\\n}\\nFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Micro Chart Facet\\n\\nThe following micro charts are supported: Area, Bullet, Radial, Column, Line, Harvey, Stacked bar and Comparison. Micro charts can only be displayed in the header. \\n\\nA micro chart is defined with the `@UI.chart` annotation, which then is the target of a ReferenceFacet in the `@UI.facet` of `purpose: #HEADER`. The title of the facet is the attribute `title` of the annotation and the subtitle is `description`. If the property of the data point is a property with unit of measure, the unit will be displayed in the footer. The attribute `measures` of the chart has to be a data point. \\n\\nThe `@UI.dataPoint` supports generally the attributes `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation`, but the support varies between the micro chart types. If the value of the data point is annotated with a unit of measure, the unit will be shown as the footer of the micro chart facet.\\n\\nIn the following examples, all used attributes are mandatory.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Area Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#AreaMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe area micro chart is a trend chart, which provides information for the actual and target value for a specified dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the dimension. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the measure attribute.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.chart: [\\n // Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n { \\n qualifier: 'areaChart',\\n title: 'Area Micro Chart (#AreaMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #AREA,\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForAreaChart'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nThe criticality calculation of the data point is mandatory, as each value is shown with its threshold (error, warning, acceptance and good) ranges.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue',\\n criticalityCalculation: { \\n improvementDirection: #TARGET,\\n toleranceRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartTolLowerboundValue',\\n toleranceRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartTolUpperboundValue',\\n deviationRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartDevUpperboundValue',\\n deviationRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartDevLowerboundValue'\\n }\\n}\\nIntegerValueForAreaChart;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'areaChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/BTP/923180ddb98240829d935862025004d6/c7f12219d533404f8fad96aa68fa4ba6.html)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Bullet Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#BulletMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe bullet chart features a single measure and compares it to one or more other measures (e.g. value with target comparison). Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. The bullet chart does not support the criticality value of 5 (new item). The `measuresAttributes`, while it is mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart, #BulletMicroChartTable\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'bulletChart',\\n title: 'Bullet Micro Chart (#BulletMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #BULLET,\\n measures: ['IntegerValue'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'IntegerValue',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'bulletChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. The attribute `minimumValue` is needed to render the chart properly. The value is the actual bar. The `forecastValue` is the bar in the background with a lower opacity and the `targetValue` is the dark line. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term: #BulletMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValue', //IntegerValue: horizontal bar in relation to the goal line\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //visual goal line in the UI\\n forecastValue: 'ForecastValue', //horizontal bar behind the value bar with, slightly larger with higher transparency\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n minimumValue: 0 //Minimal value, needed for output rendering\\n}\\nIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Radial Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#RadialMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe radial micro chart displays a single percentage value. The `measureAttributes`, while mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'radialChart',\\n title: 'Radial Micro Chart (#RadialMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #DONUT,\\n measures: ['RadialIntegerValue'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'radialChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. The percentage value is the fraction of the property value and the target value. The unit of measure label will not be rendered, as the chart displays percentage values. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //The relation between the value and the target value will be displayed as a percentage\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nRadialIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Line Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#LineMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe line chart displays a series of data points as a line. The bottom values are the border values of the `dimension`. The upper left value is the smallest value of the first `measures` property and the upper right value is the largest value of the last `measures` property. The shown unit of measure is from the first entry.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'lineChart',\\n title: 'Line Micro Chart (#LineMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #LINE,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForLineChart', 'TargetValue'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n },\\n { \\n measure: 'TargetValue',\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Points. It is recommended to only use one measure, and a maximum of three measures, if more is required. If the attribute `criticality` contains a path, then the value of the last data point's `criticality` determines the color of the line.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI: {\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'TargetValue',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n }\\n}\\nTargetValue;\\n\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForLineChart;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'lineChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Column Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#ColumnMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA column chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'columnChart',\\n title: 'Column Micro Chart (#ColumnMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #COLUMN,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'columnChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Harvey Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HarveyMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA harvey chart plots a single measure value against a maximum value.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'harveyChart',\\n title: 'Harvey Micro Chart (#HarveyMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #PIE,\\n measures: ['HarveyFieldWithPrice'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. For the semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\n\\n```\\n//Search-Term: #HarveyMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\n maximumValue: 5000,\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nHarveyFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'harveyChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Stacked Bar Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#StackedBarMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA stacked bar chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [ {\\n qualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\n title: 'StackedBar Micro Chart (#StackedBarMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #BAR_STACKED,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n}]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Comparison Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#ComparisionMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA comparison chart uses three horizontal bars to compare values of a dimension. If more values are defined in `dimensions`, they will only show up in the tooltip. The displayed values on the left represent the dimension value of each data point. The values on the right are the actual values. If a unit of measure is shown, then it is from the first data point to be plotted.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\n title: 'Comparison Micro Chart (#ComparisonMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #BAR,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. For semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Actions - Object Page Header Area\\n\\n### Copy Action in Object Page\\n\\n*Search term:* `#OPCopyAction`\\n\\n> [!WARNING] \\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\n\\n\\n\\nFor a deep copy of an instance, the RAP framework does not provide a copy action out of the box, unlike create/edit/delete. However you can define a factory action in the BDEF that copies an instance, and by using the keyword `isCopyAction: true`, the action button will be rendered in UI harmoniously with Edit/Delete.\\n\\nThe cardinality of a factory action is always [1], that means, only one instance can be copied at a time.\\n\\nWhen no label is provided, it is automatically set to 'Copy'. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.identification: [\\n // Search Term #OPCopyAction\\n {\\n type:#FOR_ACTION,\\n dataAction: 'copyInstance',\\n isCopyAction: true\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\nfactory action copyInstance [1];\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Regular Actions\\n\\n*Search term:* `#OPHeaderAction`\\n\\nActions for the Object Page in general are annotated using `@UI.identification`. The criticality only supports the values 0 (normal), 1 (red) and 3 (green). For normal actions the Object Page content is passed and for static actions, no context is passed. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #OPHeaderAction\\n@UI.identification: [\\n {\\n type: #FOR_ACTION, //Action in the RootEntities of the object page next to the edit button\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#OPHeaderAction)',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n criticalityRepresentation: #WITH_ICON,\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality'\\n }\\n]\\nID;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Action Implementation - UI Consumption of actions](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/action-implementation#ui-consumption-of-actions)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Hide Standard Operations\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DynamicCRUD`\\n\\n> [!WARNING] \\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\nThe visibility of the \\\"Edit\\\", \\\"Create\\\" and \\\"Delete\\\" buttons in the Object Page can be dynamically adjusted. For example the delete operation can be dependent on a property of the entity, through the annotation `@UI.deleteHidden`. Fixed values true or false are also possible.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #DynamicCRUD\\n@UI.updateHidden: #(UpdateHidden)\\n@UI.deleteHidden: #(DeleteHidden)\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Datafield WithAction in Object Page\\n\\n*Search term:* `#WithActionInOP`\\n\\n> [!WARNING] \\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\n\\n\\n\\nAn action that is tied to a data value, which would be rendered as a hyperlink. Therefore it is crucial to specify the annotation at the desired element which has the data value.\\n\\nThe keyword to use for this is `type: #WITH_ACTION`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // Search Term #HeaderCollectionFacet\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n id: 'FacetCollection',\\n type: #COLLECTION\\n },\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n {\\n parentId : 'FacetCollection',\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n fieldGroup: [\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithActionInOP\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n position: 30,\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithActionInOP)',\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\n }\\n ]\\n}\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n---\\n\\n*This content is from the ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE documentation. Visit the URL above for the latest version and interactive examples.*\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\",\"metadata\":{\"source\":\"abap-docs\",\"library\":\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"contentLength\":34889}}}"}

decodedResult.result: {"content":[{"type":"text","text":"{\"id\":\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"title\":\"abap-fiori-showcase > 05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"text\":\"**Source:** ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE Documentation\\n**URL:** https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\\n**File:** 05_object_page_header.md\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Object Page - Header Area\\n\\n## Content\\n- [Object Page - Header Area](#object-page---header-area)\\n - [Title and Subtitle](#title-and-subtitle)\\n - [Header Facets](#header-facets)\\n - [Header Field Group Facet](#header-field-group-facet)\\n - [Handling Semantic Key Fields](#handling-semantic-key-fields)\\n - [Plain Text](#plain-text)\\n - [Address Facet](#address-facet)\\n - [Data Points](#data-points)\\n - [Rating](#rating)\\n - [Progress](#progress)\\n -
