[Key Value](#key-value)\\n - [Micro Chart Facet](#micro-chart-facet)\\n - [Area Micro Chart](#area-micro-chart)\\n - [Bullet Micro Chart](#bullet-micro-chart)\\n - [Radial Micro Chart](#radial-micro-chart)\\n - [Line Micro Chart](#line-micro-chart)\\n - [Column Micro Chart](#column-micro-chart)\\n - [Harvey Micro Chart](#harvey-micro-chart)\\n - [Stacked Bar Micro Chart](#stacked-bar-micro-chart)\\n - [Comparison Micro Chart](#comparison-micro-chart)\\n - [Actions - Object Page Header Area](#actions---object-page-header-area)\\n - [Copy Action in Object Page](#copy-action-in-object-page)\\n - [Regular Actions](#regular-actions)\\n - [Hide Standard Operations](#hide-standard-operations)\\n - [Datafield WithAction in Object Page](#datafield-withaction-in-object-page)\\n\\n## Title and Subtitle\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderInfo`\\n\\nThe title and subtitle of an Object Page are defined with the annotation `@UI.headerInfo`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderInfo\\n@UI.headerInfo: {\\n typeName: 'Object Page - Root',\\n typeNamePlural: 'Root Entities (#ListTableTitle)', // Search Term #ListTableTitle\\n typeImageUrl: 'sap-icon://sales-order',\\n imageUrl: 'ImageUrl',\\n title: {\\n value: 'StringProperty',\\n type: #STANDARD\\n },\\n description: {\\n label: 'Root entity',\\n type: #STANDARD,\\n value: 'StringProperty'\\n }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe attribute `typeName` is the title and it is displayed next to the SAP Logo in header bar on the Object Page. The attribute `typeNamePlural` will be shown, if all entities are shown in a table. The attribute `title` is displayed as the header in the Object Page in bold. Attribute `description` is shown beneath `title` and if the optional `imageUrl` is given, then the picture will be visible on the left side of the `title` and `description`. If no url is given for the `imageUrl`, but `typeImageUrl` is defined, it will be displayed instead.\\n\\nMore Information: \\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Table Header of a List Report](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-table-header-of-list-report)\\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Title Section of an Object Page](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-title-section-of-object-page)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Header Facets\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFacet`\\n\\nThe header facet is displayed in the header of an Object Page. The facet is annotated with `UI.facet.purpose: #HEADER`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // OPHEADER\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderFacet)'\\n}\\nFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Using Facets to change the Object Page Layout](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/using-facets-to-change-object-page-layout)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Header Field Group Facet\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFieldGroup`, `#ContactInHeader`, `#OPQuickView`\\n\\nField groups can be part of a header.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nA quick view contact card can be displayed (#ContactInHeader). A QuickView can be displayed by just adding the property as a data field (#OPQuickView). Both implementations are identical to how they would be added as a line item in a List Report:\\n- [Adding a Contact Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-contact-quick-view-to-a-table)\\n- [Adding a Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-quick-view-to-a-table)\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n position: 10,\\n label: 'Field with Sem. Key(#SemanticKey)'\\n }\\n]\\nStringProperty;\\n\\n// Search Term #ContactInHeader, #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n type: #AS_CONTACT,\\n label: 'Contact QuickView (#ContactInHeader)',\\n position: 70,\\n value: '_Contact'\\n }\\n]\\nContactID;\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithAction\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n position: 20,\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithAction)',\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\n }\\n]\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #OPQuickView\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n position: 50,\\n label: 'Navigation QuickView (#OPQuickView)'\\n }\\n]\\nNavigationID;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Handling Semantic Key Fields\\n\\n*Search term:* `#SemanticKey`\\n\\nSemantic Key fields can be defined, with the annotation `@ObjectModel.semanticKey`, which consists of an array of properties from the entity. The given fields will be displayed in bold, and when possible the editing status will be displayed. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #SemanticKey\\n@ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['StringProperty']\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Plain Text\\n\\n*Search term:* `#PlainText`\\n\\nPlain text can be displayed, by adding a standard property to a field group and using this field group as a target of a reference facet.\\n\\nThe property in the field group can be optionally annotated with [`@UI.MultiLineText`](/04_object_page_general.md#multi-line-text), especially if you have a long text and would like it to be show in the UI as a multi-line text, with an expand/collapse button.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #PlainText\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n label: 'Plain Text (#PlainText)',\\n targetQualifier: 'plainText'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n // Search Term #PlainText\\n fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'plainText' } ]\\n // Search Term #MultiLineText\\n multiLineText: true\\n}\\nDescription;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Grouping Fields](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/grouping-fields)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Address Facet\\n\\n*Search term:* `#AddressFacet`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe property of an entity annotated with `@Semantics.address.label` can be directly displayed in an address facet, with target qualifier **'pref'** if no specific qualifier is set. The label will be used as is, therefore it needs to be fully formatted. Linebreaks can be achieved with '\\\\n'.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_I_Contact**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #CONTACT\\ndefine view entity /DMO/FSA_I_Contact\\n...\\n{\\n ...\\n @Semantics.address.label: true\\n @Consumption.valueHelpDefault.display:false \\n address_label as AddressLabel,\\n}\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AddressFacet\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n label : 'Address (#AddressFacet)',\\n type : #ADDRESS_REFERENCE,\\n targetElement: '_Contact',\\n targetQualifier: 'pref'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Data Points\\n\\n### Rating\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointRating`\\n\\n\\n\\nTo add a rating indicator (stars) to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets how many stars are visible. Values between x.25 and x.74 are displayed as a half star. The attribute `targetValue` defines how many stars are possible. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI: { \\n // Search Term #DataPointRating, #DataPointRatingTable\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'StarsValue',\\n targetValue: 4,\\n visualization: #RATING,\\n title: 'Rating Indicator (#DataPointRating)'\\n }\\n}\\nStarsValue;\\n```\\n\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n // Search Term #DataPointRating\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'StarsValue'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Rating](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Progress\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointProgress`\\n\\n\\n\\nTo add a progress indicator to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets the current progress and the attribute `targetValue`, the maximum progress. Additionally, a criticality can be given, if wanted. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #DataPointProgress, #DataPointProgressTable\\n@UI: {\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue',\\n targetValue: 100,\\n visualization: #PROGRESS,\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n title: 'Progress Indicator (#DataPointProgress)'\\n }\\n}\\nProgressIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n // Search Term #DataPointProgress\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Progress](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Key Value\\n\\n*Search term:* `#KeyValue`\\n\\nA key value is the default data point, when the attribute `visualization` is not specified in `@UI.dataPoint`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // Search Term #Headerfacet, #KeyValue\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderDataPoint)'\\n }\\n}\\nFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Micro Chart Facet\\n\\nThe following micro charts are supported: Area, Bullet, Radial, Column, Line, Harvey, Stacked bar and Comparison. Micro charts can only be displayed in the header. \\n\\nA micro chart is defined with the `@UI.chart` annotation, which then is the target of a ReferenceFacet in the `@UI.facet` of `purpose: #HEADER`. The title of the facet is the attribute `title` of the annotation and the subtitle is `description`. If the property of the data point is a property with unit of measure, the unit will be displayed in the footer. The attribute `measures` of the chart has to be a data point. \\n\\nThe `@UI.dataPoint` supports generally the attributes `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation`, but the support varies between the micro chart types. If the value of the data point is annotated with a unit of measure, the unit will be shown as the footer of the micro chart facet.\\n\\nIn the following examples, all used attributes are mandatory.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Area Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#AreaMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe area micro chart is a trend chart, which provides information for the actual and target value for a specified dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the dimension. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the measure attribute.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.chart: [\\n // Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n { \\n qualifier: 'areaChart',\\n title: 'Area Micro Chart (#AreaMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #AREA,\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForAreaChart'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nThe criticality calculation of the data point is mandatory, as each value is shown with its threshold (error, warning, acceptance and good) ranges.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue',\\n criticalityCalculation: { \\n improvementDirection: #TARGET,\\n toleranceRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartTolLowerboundValue',\\n toleranceRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartTolUpperboundValue',\\n deviationRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartDevUpperboundValue',\\n deviationRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartDevLowerboundValue'\\n }\\n}\\nIntegerValueForAreaChart;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'areaChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/BTP/923180ddb98240829d935862025004d6/c7f12219d533404f8fad96aa68fa4ba6.html)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Bullet Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#BulletMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe bullet chart features a single measure and compares it to one or more other measures (e.g. value with target comparison). Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. The bullet chart does not support the criticality value of 5 (new item). The `measuresAttributes`, while it is mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart, #BulletMicroChartTable\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'bulletChart',\\n title: 'Bullet Micro Chart (#BulletMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #BULLET,\\n measures: ['IntegerValue'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'IntegerValue',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'bulletChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. The attribute `minimumValue` is needed to render the chart properly. The value is the actual bar. The `forecastValue` is the bar in the background with a lower opacity and the `targetValue` is the dark line. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term: #BulletMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValue', //IntegerValue: horizontal bar in relation to the goal line\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //visual goal line in the UI\\n forecastValue: 'ForecastValue', //horizontal bar behind the value bar with, slightly larger with higher transparency\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n minimumValue: 0 //Minimal value, needed for output rendering\\n}\\nIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Radial Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#RadialMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe radial micro chart displays a single percentage value. The `measureAttributes`, while mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'radialChart',\\n title: 'Radial Micro Chart (#RadialMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #DONUT,\\n measures: ['RadialIntegerValue'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'radialChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. The percentage value is the fraction of the property value and the target value. The unit of measure label will not be rendered, as the chart displays percentage values. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //The relation between the value and the target value will be displayed as a percentage\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nRadialIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Line Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#LineMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe line chart displays a series of data points as a line. The bottom values are the border values of the `dimension`. The upper left value is the smallest value of the first `measures` property and the upper right value is the largest value of the last `measures` property. The shown unit of measure is from the first entry.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'lineChart',\\n title: 'Line Micro Chart (#LineMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #LINE,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForLineChart', 'TargetValue'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n },\\n { \\n measure: 'TargetValue',\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Points. It is recommended to only use one measure, and a maximum of three measures, if more is required. If the attribute `criticality` contains a path, then the value of the last data point's `criticality` determines the color of the line.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI: {\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'TargetValue',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n }\\n}\\nTargetValue;\\n\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForLineChart;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'lineChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Column Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#ColumnMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA column chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'columnChart',\\n title: 'Column Micro Chart (#ColumnMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #COLUMN,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'columnChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Harvey Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HarveyMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA harvey chart plots a single measure value against a maximum value.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'harveyChart',\\n title: 'Harvey Micro Chart (#HarveyMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #PIE,\\n measures: ['HarveyFieldWithPrice'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. For the semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\n\\n```\\n//Search-Term: #HarveyMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\n maximumValue: 5000,\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nHarveyFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'harveyChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Stacked Bar Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#StackedBarMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA stacked bar chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [ {\\n qualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\n title: 'StackedBar Micro Chart (#StackedBarMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #BAR_STACKED,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n}]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Comparison Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#ComparisionMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA comparison chart uses three horizontal bars to compare values of a dimension. If more values are defined in `dimensions`, they will only show up in the tooltip. The displayed values on the left represent the dimension value of each data point. The values on the right are the actual values. If a unit of measure is shown, then it is from the first data point to be plotted.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\n title: 'Comparison Micro Chart (#ComparisonMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #BAR,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. For semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Actions - Object Page Header Area\\n\\n### Copy Action in Object Page\\n\\n*Search term:* `#OPCopyAction`\\n\\n> [!WARNING] \\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\n\\n\\n\\nFor a deep copy of an instance, the RAP framework does not provide a copy action out of the box, unlike create/edit/delete. However you can define a factory action in the BDEF that copies an instance, and by using the keyword `isCopyAction: true`, the action button will be rendered in UI harmoniously with Edit/Delete.\\n\\nThe cardinality of a factory action is always [1], that means, only one instance can be copied at a time.\\n\\nWhen no label is provided, it is automatically set to 'Copy'. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.identification: [\\n // Search Term #OPCopyAction\\n {\\n type:#FOR_ACTION,\\n dataAction: 'copyInstance',\\n isCopyAction: true\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\nfactory action copyInstance [1];\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Regular Actions\\n\\n*Search term:* `#OPHeaderAction`\\n\\nActions for the Object Page in general are annotated using `@UI.identification`. The criticality only supports the values 0 (normal), 1 (red) and 3 (green). For normal actions the Object Page content is passed and for static actions, no context is passed. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #OPHeaderAction\\n@UI.identification: [\\n {\\n type: #FOR_ACTION, //Action in the RootEntities of the object page next to the edit button\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#OPHeaderAction)',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n criticalityRepresentation: #WITH_ICON,\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality'\\n }\\n]\\nID;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Action Implementation - UI Consumption of actions](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/action-implementation#ui-consumption-of-actions)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Hide Standard Operations\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DynamicCRUD`\\n\\n> [!WARNING] \\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\nThe visibility of the \\\"Edit\\\", \\\"Create\\\" and \\\"Delete\\\" buttons in the Object Page can be dynamically adjusted. For example the delete operation can be dependent on a property of the entity, through the annotation `@UI.deleteHidden`. Fixed values true or false are also possible.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #DynamicCRUD\\n@UI.updateHidden: #(UpdateHidden)\\n@UI.deleteHidden: #(DeleteHidden)\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Datafield WithAction in Object Page\\n\\n*Search term:* `#WithActionInOP`\\n\\n> [!WARNING] \\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\n\\n\\n\\nAn action that is tied to a data value, which would be rendered as a hyperlink. Therefore it is crucial to specify the annotation at the desired element which has the data value.\\n\\nThe keyword to use for this is `type: #WITH_ACTION`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // Search Term #HeaderCollectionFacet\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n id: 'FacetCollection',\\n type: #COLLECTION\\n },\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n {\\n parentId : 'FacetCollection',\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n fieldGroup: [\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithActionInOP\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n position: 30,\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithActionInOP)',\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\n }\\n ]\\n}\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n---\\n\\n*This content is from the ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE documentation. Visit the URL above for the latest version and interactive examples.*\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\",\"metadata\":{\"source\":\"abap-docs\",\"library\":\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"contentLength\":34889}}"}],"structuredContent":{"id":"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle","title":"abap-fiori-showcase > 05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle","text":"**Source:** ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE Documentation\n**URL:** https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\n**File:** 05_object_page_header.md\n\n---\n\n# Object Page - Header Area\n\n## Content\n- [Object Page - Header Area](#object-page---header-area)\n - [Title and Subtitle](#title-and-subtitle)\n - [Header Facets](#header-facets)\n - [Header Field Group Facet](#header-field-group-facet)\n - [Handling Semantic Key Fields](#handling-semantic-key-fields)\n - [Plain Text](#plain-text)\n - [Address Facet](#address-facet)\n - [Data Points](#data-points)\n - [Rating](#rating)\n - [Progress](#progress)\n - [Key Value](#key-value)\n - [Micro Chart Facet](#micro-chart-facet)\n - [Area Micro Chart](#area-micro-chart)\n - [Bullet Micro Chart](#bullet-micro-chart)\n - [Radial Micro Chart](#radial-micro-chart)\n - [Line Micro Chart](#line-micro-chart)\n - [Column Micro Chart](#column-micro-chart)\n - [Harvey Micro Chart](#harvey-micro-chart)\n - [Stacked Bar Micro Chart](#stacked-bar-micro-chart)\n - [Comparison Micro Chart](#comparison-micro-chart)\n - [Actions - Object Page Header Area](#actions---object-page-header-area)\n - [Copy Action in Object Page](#copy-action-in-object-page)\n - [Regular Actions](#regular-actions)\n - [Hide Standard Operations](#hide-standard-operations)\n - [Datafield WithAction in Object Page](#datafield-withaction-in-object-page)\n\n## Title and Subtitle\n\n*Search term:* `#HeaderInfo`\n\nThe title and subtitle of an Object Page are defined with the annotation `@UI.headerInfo`.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #HeaderInfo\n@UI.headerInfo: {\n typeName: 'Object Page - Root',\n typeNamePlural: 'Root Entities (#ListTableTitle)', // Search Term #ListTableTitle\n typeImageUrl: 'sap-icon://sales-order',\n imageUrl: 'ImageUrl',\n title: {\n value: 'StringProperty',\n type: #STANDARD\n },\n description: {\n label: 'Root entity',\n type: #STANDARD,\n value: 'StringProperty'\n }\n}\n```\n\nThe attribute `typeName` is the title and it is displayed next to the SAP Logo in header bar on the Object Page. The attribute `typeNamePlural` will be shown, if all entities are shown in a table. The attribute `title` is displayed as the header in the Object Page in bold. Attribute `description` is shown beneath `title` and if the optional `imageUrl` is given, then the picture will be visible on the left side of the `title` and `description`. If no url is given for the `imageUrl`, but `typeImageUrl` is defined, it will be displayed instead.\n\nMore Information: \n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Table Header of a List Report](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-table-header-of-list-report)\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Title Section of an Object Page](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-title-section-of-object-page)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Header Facets\n\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFacet`\n\nThe header facet is displayed in the header of an Object Page. The facet is annotated with `UI.facet.purpose: #HEADER`.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n@UI.facet: [\n // OPHEADER\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\n {\n purpose: #HEADER,\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\n }\n]\n\n// Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\n@UI.dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderFacet)'\n}\nFieldWithPrice;\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Using Facets to change the Object Page Layout](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/using-facets-to-change-object-page-layout)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Header Field Group Facet\n\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFieldGroup`, `#ContactInHeader`, `#OPQuickView`\n\nField groups can be part of a header.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\n }\n]\n```\n\n\n\nA quick view contact card can be displayed (#ContactInHeader). A QuickView can be displayed by just adding the property as a data field (#OPQuickView). Both implementations are identical to how they would be added as a line item in a List Report:\n- [Adding a Contact Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-contact-quick-view-to-a-table)\n- [Adding a Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-quick-view-to-a-table)\n\n```\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\n {\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\n position: 10,\n label: 'Field with Sem. Key(#SemanticKey)'\n }\n]\nStringProperty;\n\n// Search Term #ContactInHeader, #HeaderFieldGroup\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\n {\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\n type: #AS_CONTACT,\n label: 'Contact QuickView (#ContactInHeader)',\n position: 70,\n value: '_Contact'\n }\n]\nContactID;\n\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithAction\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\n {\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\n position: 20,\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithAction)',\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\n type: #WITH_ACTION\n }\n]\nFieldWithCriticality;\n\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #OPQuickView\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\n {\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\n position: 50,\n label: 'Navigation QuickView (#OPQuickView)'\n }\n]\nNavigationID;\n```\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Handling Semantic Key Fields\n\n*Search term:* `#SemanticKey`\n\nSemantic Key fields can be defined, with the annotation `@ObjectModel.semanticKey`, which consists of an array of properties from the entity. The given fields will be displayed in bold, and when possible the editing status will be displayed. \n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #SemanticKey\n@ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['StringProperty']\n```\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Plain Text\n\n*Search term:* `#PlainText`\n\nPlain text can be displayed, by adding a standard property to a field group and using this field group as a target of a reference facet.\n\nThe property in the field group can be optionally annotated with [`@UI.MultiLineText`](/04_object_page_general.md#multi-line-text), especially if you have a long text and would like it to be show in the UI as a multi-line text, with an expand/collapse button.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #PlainText\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER,\n type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\n label: 'Plain Text (#PlainText)',\n targetQualifier: 'plainText'\n }\n]\n\n@UI: {\n // Search Term #PlainText\n fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'plainText' } ]\n // Search Term #MultiLineText\n multiLineText: true\n}\nDescription;\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Grouping Fields](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/grouping-fields)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Address Facet\n\n*Search term:* `#AddressFacet`\n\n\n\nThe property of an entity annotated with `@Semantics.address.label` can be directly displayed in an address facet, with target qualifier **'pref'** if no specific qualifier is set. The label will be used as is, therefore it needs to be fully formatted. Linebreaks can be achieved with '\\n'.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_I_Contact**\n\n```\n// Search Term #CONTACT\ndefine view entity /DMO/FSA_I_Contact\n...\n{\n ...\n @Semantics.address.label: true\n @Consumption.valueHelpDefault.display:false \n address_label as AddressLabel,\n}\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #AddressFacet\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\n label : 'Address (#AddressFacet)',\n type : #ADDRESS_REFERENCE,\n targetElement: '_Contact',\n targetQualifier: 'pref'\n }\n]\n```\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Data Points\n\n### Rating\n\n*Search term:* `#DataPointRating`\n\n\n\nTo add a rating indicator (stars) to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets how many stars are visible. Values between x.25 and x.74 are displayed as a half star. The attribute `targetValue` defines how many stars are possible. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n@UI: { \n // Search Term #DataPointRating, #DataPointRatingTable\n dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'StarsValue',\n targetValue: 4,\n visualization: #RATING,\n title: 'Rating Indicator (#DataPointRating)'\n }\n}\nStarsValue;\n```\n\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\n\n```\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n // Search Term #DataPointRating\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'StarsValue'\n }\n]\n```\n\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Rating](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Progress\n\n*Search term:* `#DataPointProgress`\n\n\n\nTo add a progress indicator to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets the current progress and the attribute `targetValue`, the maximum progress. Additionally, a criticality can be given, if wanted. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #DataPointProgress, #DataPointProgressTable\n@UI: {\n dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue',\n targetValue: 100,\n visualization: #PROGRESS,\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\n title: 'Progress Indicator (#DataPointProgress)'\n }\n}\nProgressIntegerValue;\n```\n\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\n\n```\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n // Search Term #DataPointProgress\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue'\n }\n]\n```\n\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Progress](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Key Value\n\n*Search term:* `#KeyValue`\n\nA key value is the default data point, when the attribute `visualization` is not specified in `@UI.dataPoint`.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n@UI.facet: [\n // Search Term #Headerfacet, #KeyValue\n {\n purpose: #HEADER,\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\n }\n]\n\n@UI: {\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\n dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderDataPoint)'\n }\n}\nFieldWithPrice;\n```\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Micro Chart Facet\n\nThe following micro charts are supported: Area, Bullet, Radial, Column, Line, Harvey, Stacked bar and Comparison. Micro charts can only be displayed in the header. \n\nA micro chart is defined with the `@UI.chart` annotation, which then is the target of a ReferenceFacet in the `@UI.facet` of `purpose: #HEADER`. The title of the facet is the attribute `title` of the annotation and the subtitle is `description`. If the property of the data point is a property with unit of measure, the unit will be displayed in the footer. The attribute `measures` of the chart has to be a data point. \n\nThe `@UI.dataPoint` supports generally the attributes `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation`, but the support varies between the micro chart types. If the value of the data point is annotated with a unit of measure, the unit will be shown as the footer of the micro chart facet.\n\nIn the following examples, all used attributes are mandatory.\n\n---\n\n### Area Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#AreaMicroChart`\n\n\n\nThe area micro chart is a trend chart, which provides information for the actual and target value for a specified dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the dimension. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the measure attribute.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\n\n```\n@UI.chart: [\n // Search Term #AreaMicroChart\n { \n qualifier: 'areaChart',\n title: 'Area Micro Chart (#AreaMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #AREA,\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\n measures: ['IntegerValueForAreaChart'],\n measureAttributes: [\n {\n measure: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\n role: #AXIS_1,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n }\n]\n```\n\nThe criticality calculation of the data point is mandatory, as each value is shown with its threshold (error, warning, acceptance and good) ranges.\n\n```\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: { \n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue',\n criticalityCalculation: { \n improvementDirection: #TARGET,\n toleranceRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartTolLowerboundValue',\n toleranceRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartTolUpperboundValue',\n deviationRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartDevUpperboundValue',\n deviationRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartDevLowerboundValue'\n }\n}\nIntegerValueForAreaChart;\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER,\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'areaChart',\n targetElement: '_Chart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/BTP/923180ddb98240829d935862025004d6/c7f12219d533404f8fad96aa68fa4ba6.html)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Bullet Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#BulletMicroChart`\n\n\n\nThe bullet chart features a single measure and compares it to one or more other measures (e.g. value with target comparison). Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. The bullet chart does not support the criticality value of 5 (new item). The `measuresAttributes`, while it is mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\nChart\n\n```\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart, #BulletMicroChartTable\n@UI.chart: [\n {\n qualifier: 'bulletChart',\n title: 'Bullet Micro Chart (#BulletMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #BULLET,\n measures: ['IntegerValue'],\n measureAttributes: [\n {\n measure: 'IntegerValue',\n role: #AXIS_1,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n }\n]\n```\n\nFacet\n\n```\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER,\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'bulletChart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nData Point. The attribute `minimumValue` is needed to render the chart properly. The value is the actual bar. The `forecastValue` is the bar in the background with a lower opacity and the `targetValue` is the dark line. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\n\n```\n// Search Term: #BulletMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'IntegerValue', //IntegerValue: horizontal bar in relation to the goal line\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //visual goal line in the UI\n forecastValue: 'ForecastValue', //horizontal bar behind the value bar with, slightly larger with higher transparency\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\n minimumValue: 0 //Minimal value, needed for output rendering\n}\nIntegerValue;\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Radial Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#RadialMicroChart`\n\n\n\nThe radial micro chart displays a single percentage value. The `measureAttributes`, while mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\nChart\n\n```\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\n@UI.chart: [\n {\n qualifier: 'radialChart',\n title: 'Radial Micro Chart (#RadialMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #DONUT,\n measures: ['RadialIntegerValue'],\n measureAttributes: [\n {\n measure: 'RadialIntegerValue',\n role: #AXIS_1,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n }\n]\n```\n\nFacet\n\n```\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER, \n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'radialChart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nData Point. The percentage value is the fraction of the property value and the target value. The unit of measure label will not be rendered, as the chart displays percentage values. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\n\n```\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'RadialIntegerValue',\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //The relation between the value and the target value will be displayed as a percentage\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\n}\nRadialIntegerValue;\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Line Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#LineMicroChart`\n\n\n\nThe line chart displays a series of data points as a line. The bottom values are the border values of the `dimension`. The upper left value is the smallest value of the first `measures` property and the upper right value is the largest value of the last `measures` property. The shown unit of measure is from the first entry.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\n@UI.chart: [\n { \n qualifier: 'lineChart',\n title: 'Line Micro Chart (#LineMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #LINE,\n measures: ['IntegerValueForLineChart', 'TargetValue'],\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\n measureAttributes: [\n { \n measure: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\n role: #AXIS_2,\n asDataPoint: true\n },\n { \n measure: 'TargetValue',\n role: #AXIS_2,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n }\n]\n```\n\nData Points. It is recommended to only use one measure, and a maximum of three measures, if more is required. If the attribute `criticality` contains a path, then the value of the last data point's `criticality` determines the color of the line.\n\n```\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\n@UI: {\n dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'TargetValue',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\n }\n}\nTargetValue;\n\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: { \n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\n}\nIntegerValueForLineChart;\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER, \n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'lineChart',\n targetElement: '_Chart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Column Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#ColumnMicroChart`\n\n\n\nA column chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\n@UI.chart: [\n { \n qualifier: 'columnChart',\n title: 'Column Micro Chart (#ColumnMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #COLUMN,\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\n measureAttributes: [\n { \n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\n role: #AXIS_1,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n }\n]\n```\n\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\n\n```\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: { \n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\n}\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER, \n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'columnChart',\n targetElement: '_Chart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Harvey Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#HarveyMicroChart`\n\n\n\nA harvey chart plots a single measure value against a maximum value.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\nChart\n\n```\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\n@UI.chart: [\n {\n qualifier: 'harveyChart',\n title: 'Harvey Micro Chart (#HarveyMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #PIE,\n measures: ['HarveyFieldWithPrice'],\n measureAttributes: [\n {\n measure: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\n role: #AXIS_1,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n }\n]\n```\n\nData Point. For the semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\n\n```\n//Search-Term: #HarveyMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: {\n qualifier: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\n maximumValue: 5000,\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\n}\nHarveyFieldWithPrice;\n```\n\nFacet\n\n```\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER, \n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'harveyChart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Stacked Bar Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#StackedBarMicroChart`\n\n\n\nA stacked bar chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\n@UI.chart: [ {\n qualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\n title: 'StackedBar Micro Chart (#StackedBarMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #BAR_STACKED,\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\n measureAttributes: [\n { \n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\n role: #AXIS_1,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n}]\n```\n\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\n\n```\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: { \n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\n}\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER, \n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\n targetElement: '_Chart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Comparison Micro Chart\n\n*Search term:* `#ComparisionMicroChart`\n\n\n\nA comparison chart uses three horizontal bars to compare values of a dimension. If more values are defined in `dimensions`, they will only show up in the tooltip. The displayed values on the left represent the dimension value of each data point. The values on the right are the actual values. If a unit of measure is shown, then it is from the first data point to be plotted.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\n@UI.chart: [\n { \n qualifier: 'comparisonChart',\n title: 'Comparison Micro Chart (#ComparisonMicroChart)',\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\n chartType: #BAR,\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\n measureAttributes: [\n { \n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\n role: #AXIS_1,\n asDataPoint: true\n }\n ]\n }\n]\n```\n\nData Point. For semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\n\n```\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\n@UI.dataPoint: { \n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\n}\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\n@UI.facet: [\n {\n purpose: #HEADER, \n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'comparisonChart',\n targetElement: '_Chart'\n }\n]\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n## Actions - Object Page Header Area\n\n### Copy Action in Object Page\n\n*Search term:* `#OPCopyAction`\n\n> [!WARNING] \n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\n\n\n\nFor a deep copy of an instance, the RAP framework does not provide a copy action out of the box, unlike create/edit/delete. However you can define a factory action in the BDEF that copies an instance, and by using the keyword `isCopyAction: true`, the action button will be rendered in UI harmoniously with Edit/Delete.\n\nThe cardinality of a factory action is always [1], that means, only one instance can be copied at a time.\n\nWhen no label is provided, it is automatically set to 'Copy'. \n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n@UI.identification: [\n // Search Term #OPCopyAction\n {\n type:#FOR_ACTION,\n dataAction: 'copyInstance',\n isCopyAction: true\n }\n]\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\n\n```\nfactory action copyInstance [1];\n```\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Regular Actions\n\n*Search term:* `#OPHeaderAction`\n\nActions for the Object Page in general are annotated using `@UI.identification`. The criticality only supports the values 0 (normal), 1 (red) and 3 (green). For normal actions the Object Page content is passed and for static actions, no context is passed. \n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #OPHeaderAction\n@UI.identification: [\n {\n type: #FOR_ACTION, //Action in the RootEntities of the object page next to the edit button\n label: 'Change Criticality (#OPHeaderAction)',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\n criticalityRepresentation: #WITH_ICON,\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality'\n }\n]\nID;\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\n\n```\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\n```\n\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Action Implementation - UI Consumption of actions](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/action-implementation#ui-consumption-of-actions)\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Hide Standard Operations\n\n*Search term:* `#DynamicCRUD`\n\n> [!WARNING] \n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\n\n\n\n\n\nThe visibility of the \"Edit\", \"Create\" and \"Delete\" buttons in the Object Page can be dynamically adjusted. For example the delete operation can be dependent on a property of the entity, through the annotation `@UI.deleteHidden`. Fixed values true or false are also possible.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n// Search Term #DynamicCRUD\n@UI.updateHidden: #(UpdateHidden)\n@UI.deleteHidden: #(DeleteHidden)\n```\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n### Datafield WithAction in Object Page\n\n*Search term:* `#WithActionInOP`\n\n> [!WARNING] \n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\n\n\n\nAn action that is tied to a data value, which would be rendered as a hyperlink. Therefore it is crucial to specify the annotation at the desired element which has the data value.\n\nThe keyword to use for this is `type: #WITH_ACTION`.\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\n\n```\n@UI.facet: [\n // Search Term #HeaderCollectionFacet\n {\n purpose: #HEADER,\n id: 'FacetCollection',\n type: #COLLECTION\n },\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\n {\n parentId : 'FacetCollection',\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\n }\n]\n\n@UI: {\n fieldGroup: [\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithActionInOP\n {\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\n position: 30,\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithActionInOP)',\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\n type: #WITH_ACTION\n }\n ]\n}\nFieldWithCriticality;\n```\n\n> [!NOTE] \n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\n\n```\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\n```\n\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\n\n---\n\n---\n\n*This content is from the ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE documentation. Visit the URL above for the latest version and interactive examples.*","url":"https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area","metadata":{"source":"abap-docs","library":"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle","contentLength":34889}}}

params: {"tools":[{"name":"SAPWrite","parameters":"{\"action\":\"update\",\"lintBeforeWrite\":false,\"name\":\"ZME_DM_PROJECT\",\"source\":\"@EndUserText.label: 'UI annotations: Project'\\n@Metadata.layer: #CORE\\nannotate view ZC_DM_PROJECT with\\n{\\n  @ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['ProjectId']\\n\\n  @Search.searchable: true\\n\\n  @UI.presentationVariant: [\\n    {\\n      qualifier           : 'Standard',\\n      visualization       : #AS_LINEITEM_TABLE,\\n      sortOrder           : [{ by: 'StartDate', direction: #DESC }]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n\\n  @UI.headerInfo: {\\n {"contents":"import type { Editor } from 'mem-fs-editor';
import type { OdataService } from './types';
/**
 * Writes the odata service related data and files to an existing UI5 project specified by the base path.
 *
 * @param {string} basePath - the root path of an existing UI5 application
 * @param {OdataService} service - the OData service instance
 * @param {Editor} [fs] - the memfs editor instance
 * @throws {Error} - if required UI5 project files are not found
 * @returns {Promise<Editor>} the updated memfs editor instance
 */
export declare function generate(basePath: string, service: OdataService, fs?: Editor): Promise<Editor>;
/**
 * Writes the odata service related file updates to an existing UI5 project specified by the base path.
 *
 * @param {string} basePath - the root path of an existing UI5 application
 * @param {OdataService} service - the OData service instance
 * @param {Editor} [fs] - the memfs editor instance
 * @param {boolean} updateMiddlewares - whether the YAML files for the service (mock-server and fiori-tools-proxy middlewares) should be updated
 * @throws {Error} - if required UI5 project files are not found
 * @returns {Promise<Editor>} the updated memfs editor instance
 */
export declare function update(basePath: string, service: OdataService, fs?: Editor, updateMiddlewares?: boolean): Promise<Editor>;
/**
 * Removes service related data from project files for an existing UI5 project specified by the base path.
 * How the method works:
 * 1. Service and annotation files are removed from manifest.
 * If service type is EDMX:
 * 2. ui5.yaml
 *  - backend data of the service is removed from fiori-tools-proxy middleware
 * 3. ui5-local.yaml
 *  - backend data of the service is removed from fiori-tools-proxy middleware
 *  - service is removed from mockserver middleware
 * 4. ui5-mock.yaml
 *  - service is removed from mockserver middleware
 * If service type is CDS:
 * 2. annotations of the service are removed from CDS files.
 *
 * @param {string} basePath - the root path of an existing UI5 application
 * @param {OdataService} service - the OData service instance
 * @param {string} service.name - name of the OData service instance
 * @param {string} service.path - path of the OData service instance
 * @param {string} service.url - url of the OData service instance
 * @param {ServiceType} service.type - type of the OData service instance
 * @param {OdataService['annotations']} service.annotations - services annotations (EDMX or CDS)
 * @param {Editor} [fs] - the memfs editor instance
 * @throws {Error} - if required UI5 project files are not found
 * @returns {Promise<Editor>} the updated memfs editor instance
 */
export declare function remove(basePath: string, service: OdataService, fs?: Editor): Promise<Editor>;
export { OdataVersion, OdataService, ServiceType, EdmxAnnotationsInfo, CdsAnnotationsInfo, ExternalServiceCollectionOptions, NamespaceAlias } from './types';
export { getExternalServiceReferences, getAnnotationNamespaces, writeExternalServiceMetadata } from './data';

{"contents":"{
  "name": "dm-projects-fe",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "Fiori Elements LROP (legacy UI5 demo migration)",
  "keywords": [
    "ui5",
    "openui5",
    "sapui5"
  ],
  "main": "webapp/index.html",
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {
    "@ui5/cli": "^4.0.33",
    "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1",
    "@sapui5/types": "~1.147.0",
    "ui5-tooling-transpile": "^3.10.0",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "@sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools": "^10.0.0",
    "eslint": "^10",
    "@sap-ux/ui5-middleware-fe-mockserver": "2"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "fiori run --open \"/test/flpSandbox.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#demodmprojectsfe\"",
    "start-local": "fiori run --config ./ui5-local.yaml --open \"/test/flpSandbox.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#demodmprojectsfe\"",
    "build": "ui5 build --config=ui5.yaml --clean-dest --dest dist",
    "lint": "eslint ./",
    "ts-typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "prestart": "npm run ts-typecheck",
    "prebuild": "npm run ts-typecheck",
    "start-mock": "fiori run --config ./ui5-mock.yaml --open \"/test/flpSandbox.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false#demodmprojectsfe\"",
    "deploy": "fiori verify",
    "deploy-config": "fiori add deploy-config",
    "start-noflp": "fiori run --open \"/index.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false\"",
    "start-variants-management": "fiori run --open \"/preview.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false&fiori-tools-rta-mode=true&sap-ui-rta-skip-flex-validation=true#demodmprojectsfe\""
  },
  "sapux": true
}
","totalLinesInFile":38}

{"contents":"# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/schema/ui5.yaml.json

specVersion: "4.0"
metadata:
  name: com.demo.migration.projects.fe
type: application
framework:
  name: SAPUI5
  version: 1.147.0
  libraries:
    - name: sap.m
    - name: sap.ui.core
    - name: sap.fe.templates
    - name: sap.ushell
    - name: themelib_sap_horizon
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path: webapp
        delay: 300
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-middleware
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true
        excludePatterns:
          - /Component-preload.js
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        flp:
          theme: sap_horizon
          path: test/flpSandbox.html
          intent:
            object: comdemomigrationprojectsfe
            action: tile
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertErrors: true # If set to true, certificate errors will be ignored. E.g. self-signed certificates will be accepted
        backend:
          - path: /sap
            url: https://example-s4hana.local:50001
    - name: sap-fe-mockserver
      beforeMiddleware: csp
      configuration:
        mountPath: /
        services:
          - urlPath: /sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/srvd/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/0001
            metadataPath: ./webapp/localService/mainService/metadata.xml
            mockdataPath: ./webapp/localService/mainService/data
            generateMockData: true
        annotations: []
builder:
  customTasks:
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-task
      afterTask: replaceVersion
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true
","totalLinesInFile":66}

specVersion: "4.0"
metadata:
  name: com.demo.migration.projects.fe
type: application
framework:
  name: SAPUI5
  version: 1.147.0
  libraries:
    - name: sap.m
    - name: sap.ui.core
    - name: sap.fe.templates
    - name: sap.ushell
    - name: themelib_sap_horizon
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path: webapp
        delay: 300
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-middleware
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true
        excludePatterns:
          - /Component-preload.js
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        flp:
          theme: sap_horizon
          path: test/flpSandbox.html
          intent:
            object: comdemomigrationprojectsfe
            action: tile
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertErrors: true # If set to true, certificate errors will be ignored. E.g. self-signed certificates will be accepted
        backend:
          - path: /sap
            url: https://example-s4hana.local:50001
    - name: sap-fe-mockserver
      beforeMiddleware: csp
      configuration:
        mountPath: /
        services:
          - urlPath: /sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/srvd/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/0001
            metadataPath: ./webapp/localService/mainService/metadata.xml
            mockdataPath: ./webapp/localService/mainService/data
            generateMockData: true
        annotations: []
builder:
  customTasks:
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-task
      afterTask: replaceVersion
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true

{"contents":"# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/schema/ui5.yaml.json

specVersion: "4.0"
metadata:
  name: com.demo.migration.projects.fe
type: application
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertErrors: true # If set to true, certificate errors will be ignored. E.g. self-signed certificates will be accepted
        ui5:
          path:
            - /resources
            - /test-resources
          url: https://ui5.sap.com
        backend:
          - path: /sap
            url: https://example-s4hana.local:50001
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path: webapp
        delay: 300
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        flp:
          theme: sap_horizon
          path: test/flpSandbox.html
          intent:
            object: comdemomigrationprojectsfe
            action: tile","totalLinesInFile":52}

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/schema/ui5.yaml.json

specVersion: "4.0"
metadata:
  name: com.demo.migration.projects.fe
type: application
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertErrors: true # If set to true, certificate errors will be ignored. E.g. self-signed certificates will be accepted
        ui5:
          path:
            - /resources
            - /test-resources
          url: https://ui5.sap.com
        backend:
          - path: /sap
            url: https://example-s4hana.local:50001
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path: webapp
        delay: 300
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        flp:
          theme: sap_horizon
          path: test/flpSandbox.html
          intent:
            object: comdemomigrationprojectsfe
            action: tile

# RUN-NOTES (demo workspace)

Incremental learnings from UI5 modernization runs. Append-only sections per run.

---

## Run N — freestyle JS → TS (modern-ui5-ts-app, May 2026)

**Symptom:** `eslint webapp --fix` failed after `tsconfig.json` excluded `webapp/test/**` (tests skipped from `ts-typecheck`).

**Root cause:** `typescript-eslint` `parserOptions.project` points at `tsconfig.json`; files under `webapp/test` are no longer in the TS program, so ESLint errors with “TSConfig does not include this file”.

**Fix:** Add `webapp/test/**` to `eslint.config.mjs` `ignores` (or re-include tests in `tsconfig.json` if you want typechecked tests).

**Generic rule (proposed Self-help Pattern H):** Whenever you narrow `tsconfig` `include`/`exclude` for faster `tsc`, mirror the same exclusion in ESLint `ignores` so `npx eslint webapp` stays aligned with the TS program graph.

**SRVB URL discovery:** `SAPRead(type="SRVB", name="ZUI_DM_PROJECTS_O4")` returns binding metadata (OData V4, service definition `ZUI_DM_PROJECTS`, version `0001`) but not the HTTP path; the SRVD-direct path is confirmed by `skills/migrate-segw-to-rap.md` and matches `/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/srvd/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/0001/`.

**Action FQN:** From the same skill’s curl example: `com.sap.gateway.srvd.zui_dm_projects.v0001.approve_project` (note: service id in the namespace is `zui_dm_projects`, not `zui_dm_projects_o4`).

**UI5 manifest v2:** `routing.config.async` is rejected by `ui5-linter` (`no-removed-manifest-property`); remove it for manifest `_version` `2.0.0`.

**Proxy baseUri:** Legacy `ui5.yaml` uses `http://…:50000/sap`; modern HTTPS trial uses `https://…:50001/sap` with `strictSSL: false` and `query.sap-client: "001"`.

**Browser note:** `Claude_in_Chrome` was not available in this workspace’s MCP set; layout verification used `cursor-ide-browser` (FCL “First column” + “Middle column” regions; resizable divider). Empty list with “No projects found.” without `.env` proxy credentials is expected until `UI5_MIDDLEWARE_SIMPLE_PROXY_*` is set.

## Run N+1 — Fiori Elements LROP (`modern-fe-app/dm-projects-fe`, May 2026)

**Fiori MCP:** `execute_functionality` / `fetch-service-metadata` returned MCP **‑32600** (structured server error); fallback **@sap-ux/fiori-elements-writer** `generate()` from `modern-fe-app/dm-projects-fe/scripts/write-fe.mjs` (call `generate(basePath, config)` only—do not pass a custom **mem-fs** editor as `fs`).

**ABAP CDS UI (7.58):** **`@UI.headerInfo`**: use **`type: #STANDARD`** with **`value: 'FieldName'`** (quoted path); **`#FIELD`** / unquoted paths caused activation issues in this stack. **`@Search.defaultSearchElement`** belongs on **non-key** searchable fields only (key fields triggered activation errors). Split heavy **`@Search`** from DDLX metadata extensions into **ZC\_DM\_\*** DDLS where needed.

**DDLX `@UI.identification` FOR_ACTION:** **`criticality`** was rejected (**int** and **string**); left **`criticalityRepresentation: #WITH_ICON`** without **`criticality`** for **approve_project**.

**Local stub:** `webapp/localService/mainService/metadata.xml` is minimal hand-built OData V4 EDM for generator/offline sanity; **`scripts/validate-metadata.mjs`** uses `@sap-ux/edmx-parser`. Navigation property names (**`_TimeEntries`**) must match live **`$metadata`**—if the browser breaks at runtime, realign **`manifest.json`** navigation keys with OData.

**Manifest `_version`:** Bumping **`_version`** to **`2.0.0`** broke **`run_manifest_validation`** (for example **`odataVersion`**, **`customType`**); app stays at **`1.85.0`** until those align with tooling.
